items.title: Concept map of NumPy vectorization
items.mermaid:
mindmap
  root(("NumPy vectorization"))
    ops("Whole-array ops")
      ufuncs("Ufuncs: add, exp, clip")
      reductions("Reductions: mean, sum")
    loops("vs Python loops")
      interpreter("Interpreter per element")
      compiled("Compiled C/Fortran kernels")
    broadcast("Broadcasting")
      align("Align trailing dims")
      stretch("Stretch size-1 dims")
    perf("ML prep performance")
      layout("Contiguous dtypes")
      masks("Boolean masks")
      profile("Profile realistic sizes")
items.caption: Vectorization combines whole-array ufuncs and reductions, broadcasting for shape alignment, and performance habits for ML data pipelines.

items.title: Broadcasting alignment flow
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A["Array A shape (m, n)"] --> compare["Compare trailing dimensions"]
  B["Array B shape (n,) or (1, n)"] --> compare
  compare --> ok["Equal or one side is 1"]
  compare --> fail["Sizes differ and neither is 1"]
  ok --> result["Element-wise result shape (m, n)"]
  fail --> error["ValueError: operands could not be broadcast"]
items.caption: NumPy walks shapes from the right: compatible dimensions broadcast; incompatible ones raise an error instead of silently guessing.
